paper_title: Modeling and Classification of Coastal Sediments and Environments

methods.description: A 1-D reactive transport model for coastal sediments is developed using the Biogeochemical Reaction Network Simulator to simulate mixed kinetic-equilibrium reaction networks describing early diagenesis.

steps.description: Develop a 1-D reactive transport model using the Biogeochemical Reaction Network Simulator.
steps.searchable_summary: develop 1-D reactive transport model using Biogeochemical Reaction Network Simulator

steps.description: Model vertical concentration fields in the sediment using 1-D advection-diffusion-reaction equations.
steps.searchable_summary: model vertical concentration fields using advection-diffusion-reaction equations

steps.description: Solve a discretized version of the equations with an operator splitting approach for transport and reaction terms.
steps.searchable_summary: solve discretized equations using operator splitting approach

steps.description: Discretize solute transport using the Crank-Nicholson algorithm.
steps.searchable_summary: discretize solute transport using Crank-Nicholson algorithm

steps.description: Use a flux limiting total variation diminishing scheme for solids and sorbed species.
steps.searchable_summary: apply flux limiting total variation diminishing scheme

steps.description: Cast the reaction term as a set of differential algebraic equations (DAEs) and solve using a Newton algorithm.
Impose a 5 × 10 −6 yr time step for transient simulations until steady state.
steps.searchable_summary: impose time step for transient simulations

steps.description: Discretize the modeled sediment thickness of 50 cm into 116 irregularly spaced nodes.

methods.description: A classification of the coastal ocean into different environments with broadly similar characteristics, organized according to a series of hierarchically related system levels.

steps.description: Divide the coastal ocean into distinct environments characterized by a distinct mode of carbonate production and accumulation.
steps.searchable_summary: classify coastal ocean into environments based on carbonate production

steps.description: Subdivide environmental units into regions characterized by similar controls on carbonate dissolution.
steps.searchable_summary: subdivide environments based on carbonate dissolution controls

steps.description: Extract average environmental conditions from literature or the Levitus94 database.
steps.searchable_summary: extract environmental conditions from literature or database

steps.description: Use water depth to further classify environments at Level III.
steps.searchable_summary: classify environments based on water depth
